# Tracking Spending

<br>

You only manage what you measure. This chapter covers how to capture every
dollar that leaves your accounts and turn that data into insight.

  - Categorizing transactions
  - Reviewing on a monthly cadence
  - Spotting "leak" categories (subscriptions, delivery fees, impulse buys)

## Online Subscriptions: The Silent Drain

This is the most overlooked category in personal finance. Streaming, apps, software, memberships, deliveries; they're individually small but collectively enormous.

```{=html}
<style>

.codeStyle span:not(.nodeLabel) {
  font-family: monospace;
  font-size: 1.5em;
  font-weight: bold;
  color: #9753b8 !important;
  background-color: #f6f6f6;
  padding: 0.2em;
}
</style>
```

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: 'Subscription Audit'
%%| fig-align: center
%%{init: {'theme': 'neutral', 'look': 'handDrawn', 'themeVariables': { 'fontFamily': 'monospace', "fontSize":"16px"}}}%%

flowchart TD
    Subscriptions[Monthly Subscriptions] --> Audit[Quarterly Audit]
    Audit --> Use[Used in last 30 days?]
    Use -->|Yes| Worth[Worth the cost?]
    Use -->|No| Cancel[Cancel Today]
    Worth -->|Yes| Keep[Keep]
    Worth -->|No| Cancel
    Cancel --> Redirect[Redirect to Investments]
```

### The Subscription Reality Check {.unnumbered}

The average household spends **$200–$300/month** on subscriptions, often without realizing it. At $250/month invested at a 7% real return over 30 years, that's roughly **$300,000 in lost wealth**.

**Action steps:**

1. Pull your last 3 months of bank/card statements
2. List every recurring charge
3. Cancel anything you haven't actively used in 30 days
4. Set a calendar reminder every 90 days to repeat this
5. Use one card exclusively for subscriptions to track them easily

This isn't deprivation; it's the Sethi principle in action: **cut mercilessly on what you don't love** so you can spend freely on what you do.

### The Subscription Audit Math {.unnumbered}

A simple but powerful calculation.

**Formula:** Monthly Subscription × 12 × (1.07)^n = Opportunity cost in *n* years

Example: A $20/month subscription you don't really use, over 30 years:

- Annual cost: $240
- Invested at 7% real return for 30 years: **~$24,000**

That "small" $20/month is a $24,000 decision. **Audit accordingly.**
